source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/04_제품별 인허가 진행 문서/04_2G & 3G (A1417MCW, A1717MCW, F1417MCW)/▶ CE MDR/230926 기술문서 & 임상평가 보완 리뷰 2 (with 컨설팅)/CE MDR TD 보완_230926/A-TCF-5-155 Risk Management/A-TCF-5-104 Risk Management Report (rev.2).docx

sourceSummary.oneLine: Risk management report for H&abyz Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector models A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Documents risk management activities, risk acceptability, risk control, residual risk evaluation, and production/post-production information for the device.
keyPoints: The device is a Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector classified as IIa with an expected service life of approximately 5 years.
The report cites EU Regulation 2017/745 and the Medical Device Act in Korea as applicable national and regional regulations.
EN ISO 14971:2019 is applied for risk management, with acceptable risk levels defined as 1 to 4 and unacceptable risk levels as 5 to 25.
Before risk control, 40 risks were identified, with 2 acceptable and 38 unacceptable.
After risk control measures, residual risks were stated to be reduced to the acceptable region.
Production and post-production information collection includes customer satisfaction, complaints, inspection results, standards monitoring, design changes, and adverse event reporting if necessary.

entities.name: H&abyz Co., Ltd.
entities.evidence: This is a document for the H&abyz Co., Ltd. to establish and document a risk management plan in accordance with the risk management procedure.

entities.name: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector
entities.evidence: Product name: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

entities.name: European Union
entities.evidence: European Union Regulation (EU) 2017/745 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 5 April 2017

entities.name: Republic of Korea
entities.evidence: Republic of Korea Medical Device Act in Korea

concepts.name: Risk management
concepts.evidence: systematic application of management policies, procedures (3.13) and practices to the tasks of analysing, evaluating, controlling and monitoring risk

concepts.name: Risk management file
concepts.evidence: set of records (3.16) and other documents that are produced by risk management

concepts.name: Risk acceptability criteria
concepts.evidence: Acceptable Risk (A): Acceptable Region, Green Zone, Risk levels: 1~4 Unacceptable Risk (U): Unacceptable Region, Red Zone, Risk levels: 5~25

concepts.name: Residual risk evaluation
concepts.evidence: After performing risk control measures, evaluate and record residual risks according to the criteria defined in the risk management plan.

concepts.name: Benefit-risk analysis
concepts.evidence: The benefit-risk analysis is used to determine if the residual risk is outweighed by the expected benefits of the intended use of the medical device.

concepts.name: Production and post-production information
concepts.evidence: activities related to collection and review of relevant production and post-production information.

concepts.name: Essential performance
concepts.evidence: Manufacturer shall identify which functions are Essential Performance.

concepts.name: Reasonably foreseeable misuse
concepts.evidence: Might be misused by maintenance and calibration error of operator.

concepts.name: Post-market surveillance
concepts.evidence: Refer to A-TCF-5-161 Post-Market Surveillance for getting the information of production and post- production phases.

evidence.claim: The report covers Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector models A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW.
evidence.evidenceText: Product name: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Model name: A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW

evidence.claim: The current revision identified in the revision history is revision 2.
evidence.evidenceText: Revision No. 2 날짜 Response to 1st review by 3EC (NB)

evidence.claim: Revision 1 applied EN ISO 14971:2019.
evidence.evidenceText: 1 Dec/02/2022 Applied EN ISO 14971:2019

evidence.claim: The document lists EU Regulation 2017/745 and the Medical Device Act in Korea as applicable regulations.
evidence.evidenceText: European Union Regulation (EU) 2017/745 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 5 April 2017 Republic of Korea Medical Device Act in Korea

evidence.claim: The device is classified as IIa.
evidence.evidenceText: Classification IIa

evidence.claim: The expected service life is approximately 5 years.
evidence.evidenceText: Expected service life Approximately 5 years

evidence.claim: The intended purpose is general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children.
evidence.evidenceText: The A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.

evidence.claim: The device is intended to replace film based radiographic diagnostic systems.
evidence.evidenceText: It is intended to replace film based radiographic diagnostic systems and provide more precise case diagnosis and treatment planning on a real time basis for physicians and radiologists.

evidence.claim: The detector can operate as a wired or wireless digital flat panel detector.
evidence.evidenceText: The A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW are wired or wireless digital flat panel detectors that have been designed for faster, more streamlined approach to digital radiography systems.

evidence.claim: The detector consists of TFT, CsI scintillator, and electronics converting read data into digital signals.
evidence.evidenceText: The detector consists of TFT and Scintillator (CsI) and an electronic device that converts data read through it into digital signals.

evidence.claim: Essential performance includes obtaining the acquisition image as intended and avoiding two adjacent lines or blank acquired images.
evidence.evidenceText: Acquisition image is obtained as intended. The acquired image should not have two lines adjacent or be blank.

evidence.claim: The report defines acceptable risk levels as 1 to 4 and unacceptable risk levels as 5 to 25.
evidence.evidenceText: Acceptable Risk (A): Acceptable Region, Green Zone, Risk levels: 1~4 Unacceptable Risk (U): Unacceptable Region, Red Zone, Risk levels: 5~25

evidence.claim: Before risk control, 40 risks were identified and estimated.
evidence.evidenceText: Through the identification of known and foreseeable hazards, 40 risks were identified and estimated.

evidence.claim: Before risk control, 2 risks were acceptable and 38 were unacceptable.
evidence.evidenceText: Acceptable region: 2ea Unacceptable region: 38ea

evidence.claim: Risk reduction is required for risks in the Red Zone.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk reduction is required for the risks that belong to the Red Zone.

evidence.claim: Risk control options are prioritized as inherently safe design and manufacture, protective measures, and information for safety or training.
evidence.evidenceText: We used one or more of the following risk control options in priority order listed below... inherently safe design and manufacture... protective measures in the medical device itself or in the manufacturing process... information for safety and, where appropriate, training to users

evidence.claim: After risk control, all identified residual risks were evaluated in an acceptable region.
evidence.evidenceText: All identified residual risks evaluated after implementation of the risk control measures, are distributed in an acceptable region.

evidence.claim: The report states all risks are controlled by adequate risk control measures in accordance with the risk management plan.
evidence.evidenceText: All risks are controlled by adequate risk control measures in accordance with risk management plan.

evidence.claim: Overall residual risks are determined to be acceptable through warning, operating method, adverse event reporting, and usability expert review.
evidence.evidenceText: Finally, the overall residual risks are determined to be acceptable through the warning, operating method and adverse event reporting and usability expert review.

evidence.claim: Production and post-production information is to be obtained through post-market surveillance document A-TCF-5-161.
evidence.evidenceText: Refer to A-TCF-5-161 Post-Market Surveillance for getting the information of production and post- production phases.

evidence.claim: Quality Assurance collects and reviews customer satisfaction, complaints, inspection and test results, and measuring device monitoring.
evidence.evidenceText: Quality Assurance 1. Customer satisfaction survey... 2. Statistical processing of customer complaints... 3. Monitoring results of the incoming inspection and end product test results... 4. Monitoring the measuring devices

evidence.claim: R&D activities include design changes, monitoring national and international standards, and collecting and analyzing market information.
evidence.evidenceText: R&D 1. Design changes... 2. Monitoring the national and international standards... 3. Collect and analysis of the market information

evidence.claim: The Quality manager is responsible for collecting, reviewing, and managing quality problems in production and post-production phases.
evidence.evidenceText: Responsibility and the role of the Quality manager Collect, review the information and manage the quality problems in production and post-production phase

evidence.claim: Risk analysis for collected information is performed once every 12 months.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk analysis for the collected information (once every 12 months)

evidence.claim: If necessary, the Quality manager reports safety information and side effects to the regulatory agency.
evidence.evidenceText: According to the seriousness of the information collected, the Quality manager shall report safety information and any side effects to the regulatory agency (Applicable national or regional regulations).

reviewFlags.reason: Current revision date is shown as Korean placeholder text '날짜'; no final document date is present.
Source metadata authoritySignal is MFDS/domestic, but source path and document content also indicate CE MDR/EU Regulation 2017/745 context.
Approver, reviewer, and preparer dates are placeholders rather than completed dates.
The report states after-control region totals as Acceptable 40, Unacceptable 0, Total 38, which is internally inconsistent.
Text contains minor OCR or transcription issues, including misspellings such as 'corraective', 'vaildation', and inconsistent standard reference 'EN 15322-1' versus listed EN 15223-1.
